name: Satay Beef- Nuea Satay
recipeInstructions:
Satays are one of Thailand's most popular appetizers.
1. In a large bowl, mix together all the ingredients for the marinade. Dip each piece of meat in the sauce and set aside. Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
2. Weave each piece of meat onto an 8-inch skewer lengthwise.
3. Broil or grill the satays for 5 minutes on each side, or pan-fry them. To pan-fry:
a. Brush a large non-stick pan with coconut milk or leftover marinade.
b. Pan-fry the meat for 2 to 3 minutes on each side.
c. Brush the meat with the sauce as it is turned.
4. Serve with peanut sauce (Nam Jim Satay).
